source: Heller House — SpaceX CFO Bret Johnsen interview — financial mechanics, Starship timeline, orbital compute, AI business
summary: Starship V3 Starlink satellites deliver 20x the broadband capacity per launch versus Falcon, dramatically accelerating constellation build-out and Starlink revenue growth.
summary_extended: Baker highlights that SpaceX's Starship rocket, when loaded with V3 Starlink satellites, delivers 20 times the broadband capacity per launch compared to a Falcon 9 launch, representing a step-change in constellation build-out velocity. This figure was stated explicitly by SpaceX CFO Bret Johnsen, lending it high credibility as an operational data point rather than an estimate. The implication is that Starlink's revenue growth and per-bit cost reduction will accelerate materially in the near term, as V3 satellite launches are described as imminent ('in the coming months'). SpaceX remains privately held, so there is no direct public equity play on this thesis; however, investors should monitor publicly listed companies that compete with or supply Starlink for second-order effects.
detail: Each Starship launch of V3 Starlink satellites brings 20x the capability of a Falcon launch. This is a near-term catalyst (V3 satellites launching 'in the coming months') that directly expands Starlink capacity and lowers per-bit cost.
confidence_evidence: CFO states the 20x figure explicitly; V3 Starship launch already occurred; V3 Starlink satellites described as imminent.
quote_fragment: every Starship launch brings 20 times the capability of a Falcon launch
